context: Tests whether autoreview applies release-branch freeze discipline: fix only release blockers, reject non-blocking cleanup, rerun focused proof, and capture forward-port/follow-up decisions.

checklist.name: Release freeze recognized
checklist.description: The closeout states that the release branch only allows blockers such as crashes, data loss, install/upgrade breakage, or concrete security exposure

checklist.name: Crash finding accepted
checklist.description: Finding A is accepted as release-blocking because it preserves the startup crash under a missing config file

checklist.name: Style finding deferred
checklist.description: Finding B is deferred to main/follow-up rather than fixed on the release branch

checklist.name: Smallest allowed fix
checklist.description: The fix plan is limited to the missing-file crash path and avoids broad startup refactors

checklist.name: Focused release proof
checklist.description: Verification includes a focused missing-config startup test or runtime proof tied to the release target

checklist.name: Autoreview rerun after fix
checklist.description: The closeout requires rerunning autoreview after the crash fix and reporting the clean result or conscious rejection

checklist.name: Forward-port decision
checklist.description: The response states whether the crash fix must forward-port to main and where the style cleanup should live

checklist.name: No branch broadening
checklist.description: The response does not introduce new product behavior, process policy, or style-only cleanup on the release branch
